summary: Matched the coin item implementation in `src/melee/it/items/itcoin.c` with a small set of code-shaping changes: extracted repeated JObj rotation clearing into an inline helper, adjusted stack padding, and introduced a local `f32 zero` in velocity/position comparisons and assignments to influence codegen.
what_changed: Added inline helper `itCoin_ResetRotation(Item_GObj* gobj)` that gets the item's `HSD_JObj` via `GET_JOBJ(gobj)` and sets X/Y/Z rotation to `0.0F`.
Replaced duplicated direct `HSD_JObjSetRotationX/Y/Z` sequences in `it_802F1630` and `it_802F1CE0` with calls to `itCoin_ResetRotation(gobj)`.
Removed the local `HSD_JObj* jobj` from `it_802F1630` because the reset helper now handles JObj lookup there.
Changed `it_802F1630` stack padding to remain at `PAD_STACK(16)` after the helper extraction.
In `it_802F1910`, introduced `f32 zero = 0.0F` and used it for comparisons against `ip->x40_vel.x`, `ip->x40_vel.y`, `ip->pos.x`, and for assigning `ip->x40_vel.z`.
Changed `it_802F1CE0` stack padding from `PAD_STACK(24)` to `PAD_STACK(16)` while calling the shared rotation reset helper.
smart_moves: Factoring the three rotation-zeroing calls into an inline helper let two call sites share the same source shape while still allowing the compiler to inline it for matching.
Using a local `f32 zero` instead of repeated `0.0F` literals in `it_802F1910` is a deliberate codegen tactic for floating-point comparison/assignment matching.
Stack padding was tuned alongside the source refactor, showing that helper extraction and local variable choices were made with emitted stack layout in mind rather than just readability.
The helper name `itCoin_ResetRotation` documents the behavior without changing the surrounding unresolved function names such as `it_802F1630` and `it_802F1CE0`.
decomp_lessons: For item object rotation resets, an `inline` helper that performs `GET_JOBJ` and three `HSD_JObjSetRotation*` calls can be useful when the original code appears to reuse a common pattern.
When matching PowerPC floating-point code, replacing repeated float literals with a local `f32 zero` can affect register/local handling enough to close a mismatch.
Small stack differences may require coordinated changes to `PAD_STACK` values after extracting helpers or introducing/removing locals.
Do not assume readability-only refactors are harmless in matched files; even repeated `0.0F` literals versus a local variable may matter for output.
naming_conventions: New helper uses item-specific CamelCase-style prefix: `itCoin_ResetRotation`.
Existing unresolved functions retained address-style names such as `it_802F1604`, `it_802F1630`, `it_802F1910`, `it_802F1CB4`, and `it_802F1CE0`.
Existing engine/API names retain project conventions: `Item_GObj`, `HSD_JObj`, `GET_JOBJ`, `HSD_JObjSetRotationX`, `HSD_JObjSetRotationY`, `HSD_JObjSetRotationZ`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduce `void inline` helper to preserve an inlined shared code pattern while changing C source structure.
Use a local `f32 zero = 0.0F` for multiple float comparisons and assignments in one function.
Tune `PAD_STACK` manually after local/helper changes, specifically `PAD_STACK(24)` to `PAD_STACK(16)` in `it_802F1CE0`.

Move JObj lookup into the inline helper for `it_802F1630`; `it_802F1CE0` still shows a local `HSD_JObj* jobj = GET_JOBJ(gobj)` in the diff context even after switching the rotation reset to the helper, suggesting a source-shape/codegen consideration, though the PR context does not explain it.
follow_up_queries: Find other item files that clear JObj rotation with three `HSD_JObjSetRotation*` calls and may benefit from an inline reset helper for matching.
Search for functions comparing multiple floats to `0.0F` where introducing a local `f32 zero` may improve codegen.
Inspect remaining `itcoin.c` unmatched functions around `it_802F1910` for similar stack-padding and local-variable matching tactics.
